requirements.background:
Currently commissioned arts are organized by folders
``` . ├── Chibi │ ├── chibi-background-banzhishouza.jpg │ ├── chibi-banzhishouza.png │ ├── chibi-gengzi-1.png │ ├── chibi-gengzi-2.png │ ├── chibi-with-dileselon.png │ ├── qrcode.png │ ├── qrcode.psd │ └── qrcode.sai2 ├── Other │ ├── cut-tail.png │ ├── totem.png │ ├── weapon-greatsword-watermark.jpg │ ├── weapon-greatsword.png │ └── ... ├── Monochrome │ └── ... └── ... ```
As the number of commission art grows, the following problems have emerged 1. An image may belong to multiple categories An avatar may be both an avatar and monochrome Since the filesystem is tree-structured, an image can only belong to one category folder, which is clearly unreasonable 2. Titles contain irrelevant information Some metadata is mixed into titles, such as whether it has a background, the artist, co-commission partner, etc. Metadata needs to be preserved, but mixing it into titles reduces title readability 3. An image may exist in multiple formats 4. The way images are added, managed, and previewed is quite primitive A better method for managing commission art is needed, including - Adding and deleting - Metadata - Multi-dimensional filtering and categorization - Preview - Different resolution export - \[Optional\] Extract `psd` layers for export - \[Optional\] Physical watermark / digital watermark - \[Optional\] CLIP - `image2txt` image accessibility - \[Optional\] Safe mode
requirements.reference: [Project link](https://ryuhane.notion.site/8cc55c3a72c94832983b36bc514a8f7b?v=a4b1ded8480d458297966e7fc3349f02) Notion-based commission management Consists of two parts: settings and commissions. Settings include characters, outfits, weapons, etc. Commissions support preview, search, filtering, and sorting
requirements.goals:
Implement a commission management application that meets Heiyao's needs The core is commission art management; settings and commission inspiration are currently managed with Obsidian and that is sufficient
Features to implement - Images, metadata - Preview, export - Search, filter, sort - Authentication for edit access

entities.metadata: Title Description Time - the completion time of the commission, precise to the day Labels - multi-select, unified label system covering categories, tags, and rating: - Category - predefined labels for image type, e.g. Chibi, Avatar, etc. - Tag - freely defined labels - Rating - exactly one required per commission; predefined values: General, Mature, Adult - Label types differ only in frontend display semantics; the backend stores all labels identically Characters - multi-select, see Characters Artists - multi-select, see Artists Cover image - small image Files - a commission contains nodes, each node contains files. Files may be any format: rendered images (png, jpg), source files (psd, sai2), design documents, etc. - Each file has a format, an optional label (e.g. "with background", "差分 A"), and a flag indicating whether it is a displayable image - Displayable image files additionally store resolution (width × height) and a focal point for thumbnail cropping - Only displayable image files are eligible to be used as cover art
entities.character: Name - the character's name; kept as a first-class field to support DB-level search and filtering Settings - an XML blob for all other character attributes (species, appearance, lore, etc.); parsed by the frontend Note: species filtering (see Search, Filter, Sort) is expected to operate on the parsed XML at the application layer, not the DB layer
entities.artist: Name - the artist's name; kept as a first-class field to support DB-level search and filtering Info - an XML blob for all other artist attributes (contact links, social handles, notes, etc.); parsed by the frontend
entities.lifecycle:
A commission progresses through a series of stages tracked as ordered nodes. Admins can freely define and reorder nodes.
Nodes are linear and sequential. Each node has: - Name - the stage name, e.g. "Sketching", "Lineart", "Delivered" - Order - explicit integer position to preserve admin-defined ordering - Started at - timestamp recording when this stage began - Files - any files received at this stage (rendered images, source files, design docs, etc.) Each commission also has exactly one system-managed **detached node**: - Automatically created when the commission is created; cannot be deleted or reordered - When a regular node is deleted, all its associated images are reassigned to the detached node - Images in the detached node are considered uncategorized and should be visually distinguished in the UI
Commission-level lifecycle fields (stored on commission_metadata): - Confirmed at - timestamp when the commission was accepted/confirmed by the admin - Price amount - the agreed commission price as a decimal - Price currency - ISO 4217 currency code, e.g. "USD", "JPY", "CNY"

design.storage: All commission metadata is stored uniformly in a single database. Image files are managed through an abstracted storage layer that decouples the database from any specific storage backend. - The initial supported backend is the local filesystem - The design must allow future backends (e.g. S3, GCS) to be added without schema changes - Each image file is represented as a storage object with a backend type, an optional container/bucket, and a backend-specific key - The concept of a "commission folder" is a convention of the local backend, not a database-level constraint
design.export:
Both export modes are privileged operations that require edit access. Two export modes are supported. Used when migrating between database or application installations. - No physical files are exported - Exports the full database contents (commissions, metadata, nodes, labels, characters, artists, storage object records, etc.) - Storage object records retain their original backend type, bucket, and key, so files remain resolvable after the DB is imported into a new installation provided the storage backend is accessible at the same location - Intended for full application migration, not for end-user file delivery
Exports physical files packaged as a `.zip` archive, prompted for download once the archive is ready. - Directory layout inside the zip: `{commission_artists}-{commission_id}/{node_name}/` - `commission_artists` is derived from the names of all artists linked to the commission, e.g. `artistA-artistB-42` - Each node becomes a subdirectory named after the node; the detached node is included if it contains files - File timestamps: - The commission directory's creation time is set to the date of the most recently started node (`started_at`) - Each node directory's creation time is set to that node's `started_at` date - Only physical files are exported; no metadata files are generated

ux-and-ui.pages:
Pages Should mainly include: home page, add/edit page, detail page
The home page is similar to a photo album app design, mainly used to display the commission list - Can add a commission -> navigates to the add commission page - Can operate on a commission - Can select multiple commissions for batch operations - Can click a commission -> navigates to the commission detail page - Can filter, search, and sort to change the currently displayed commission list
Mainly used to display commission details - Display commission full-size image - Display commission metadata - Can operate on the commission - Can edit or delete the commission -> edit navigates to the edit page, delete navigates to the home page
Similar to the detail page - Can add or remove commission full-size images - Can edit commission metadata - Can cancel or complete -> navigates to the parent page - Should validate data validity
ux-and-ui.image-layout:
The image layout leans toward referencing [FurAffinity](https://www.furaffinity.net)
All images have the same width and height. Reference: [Natsume Ryuhane](#natsume-ryuhane) Pros - Clean and uniform layout Cons - Thumbnails of extreme aspect ratio images will be heavily cropped
Multi-row or multi-column layout; each row has equal height or each column has equal width, extending infinitely in one direction. Reference: [Pinterest](https://www.pinterest.com) Pros - Adapts to images of various sizes without cropping - High space utilization
Cons - Displayed images are unordered - Portrait images in a horizontal layout or landscape images in a vertical layout will appear smaller - May lose the focal point during preview
Manually adjust the size and position of each image based on importance, dimensions, etc., achieving a photo wall-like effect Pros - Carefully designed layout with good visual effect
Cons - Displayed images are unordered - Only suitable for a limited number of images; cannot add, delete, or reorder without re-laying out
1. The layout flows left-to-right, top-to-bottom -> preserves order 2. Equal spacing between images; all images within a column have equal height -> the trade-off is that each column's height fluctuates within a certain range
ux-and-ui.search-filter-sort:
Whether searching, filtering, or sorting, all operate on a proper subset of all commissions Search filters the full set of commissions Search can be exact or fuzzy
Search types - Search by title - Search by description - \[Optional\] Search by image content
Search can target a single type or multiple types
Filter operates on the full set of commissions or on search results Filtering is exact
Filter types - Filter by category - categories are searchable - Filter by tag - tags are searchable - Filter by rating - Filter by time range - Filter by character count range - Filter by character species - species are searchable - Filter by character or artist name - names are searchable - Filter by image format
Filter types can be stacked, and within a specific type multiple values can be selected, e.g. filter by multiple tags
Sort determines the display order of commissions
Sort has ascending and descending Sort has two dimensions - By commission completion time - By commission title alphabetical order - needs to account for pinyin

other.thumbnails: Should allow users to specify the main subject area of an image to generate correct thumbnails, ensuring proper cropping
other.catrgories-and-tags: Categories and tags should only differ semantically, not in implementation
